hooks.command: osascript -e 'display notification "Claude is waiting for input" with title "Claude Code" sound name "Glass"'
osascript -e 'display notification "Action Required: Yes/No" with title "Claude Code Permission" sound name "Hero"'

environment.global_claude_md:
# Jacob's global Claude rules (cross-session)

These apply to every Claude Code session on this Mac. Project-specific `CLAUDE.md` files override these where they conflict.

Last updated: 2026-05-11.

---

## 1. Prefer principles over rules; nudge, don't prescribe

When tempted to enumerate every case ("if X then A, if Y then B, if Z then C…"), stop and ask: can the next Claude agent figure this out from a one-sentence principle plus context? Usually yes.

Over-specified rules age badly, crowd out attention from the rules that actually matter, and assume cases you can't predict. Trust the working Claude's in-context judgment. The smallest nudge that produces the right behavior is the right rule.

This principle governs how every other rule in this file should be read: when a rule lists examples, treat them as illustrative, not exhaustive.

---

## 2. Quality > speed; depth > token count

Be really excessive on planning, thinking, literature review, and online search. Jacob cares a lot about quality, more than turnaround time, and tokens are not a constraint.

- Solve at the root, not the symptom.
- Think mid-to-long term. Strong foundations beat fast patches.
- Don't rush. If a problem deserves more search/planning/verification, take it.
- When there's a chance to verify-and-loop, follow it; don't short-circuit because "it's taking too many loops." It's very much encouraged to be meticulous.

When in doubt about whether to cut a corner: don't.

---

## 3. Render before review (HTML default; .docx for comment loops)

**Strict preference: produce a report.** When Claude makes changes in a session — even small ones — Jacob wants a rendered report by default, not just chat output. Silent execution is the wrong default; when in doubt, render. This is a strict preference, not "render if convenient."

Never point Jacob at raw source (`.md`, `.tex`, `.bib`, `.json`, etc.) when he needs to read, review, or sign off. Render first.

- **Default: HTML.** Write a file, `open` it in his browser. Fast, expressive, no toolchain. For interactive review artifacts that evolve across many renders (audits, proposals, decision queues), invoke the `html-review-builder` skill for current conventions.
- **If Jacob will leave inline / margin comments:** use `.docx` (e.g., `commented-edit-roundtrip`, `revision-queue`).
- **Anything else** (PDF for paper-stage manuscripts, images for visualizations, slides for presentations, inline code blocks for short snippets, etc.) — match format to purpose, your call.

When the rendered file changes substantially, reopen it (`open <file>`) without being asked. The rendered artifact is the primary channel for non-trivial content; keep chat tight, put detail in the report.

Internal state files, logs, and skill-machinery files don't need rendering unless Jacob asks.

---

## 4. Filesystem conventions: source folders, navigation, AI authorship

### `(source)` / `[source]` — read-only reference material

Read freely, cite freely. Editing, renaming, deleting, or extracting *into* them requires explicit per-action permission, requested with the magic phrase **"extract here"** so Jacob doesn't auto-grant by reflex. If you find content that *should* be flagged as source but isn't, don't rename — record it in the project's `MAP.md` instead.

### `(claude)` suffix — flags AI-generated content awaiting human review

Apply to whole folders of deliverables (`lit_review (claude)/`) or standalone proposal files. Manuscripts use `_PROPOSED` instead. Don't suffix mixed-authorship files (record authorship in `MAP.md` Provenance) or convention-mandated filenames (`CLAUDE.md`, `README.md`, `MAP.md`, `HANDOFF.md`).

### `MAP.md` — required per project

Every project should have a `MAP.md` at its root: folder structure with one-line descriptions, source/claude flagging, Provenance section for non-obvious authorship, what's shareable vs. local-only. If missing, create one during first orientation — the `project-map` skill defines the convention. Subfolders MAY have their own `MAP.md` only when complex enough to warrant separate orientation; location encodes scope.

Renames in git-tracked folders need explicit OK before applying — they change git history.

---

## 5. Operating notes

- macOS, bash/zsh. Claude Code spawns non-interactive shells that don't source `~/.zshrc` — put env changes in `~/.zshenv` or prepend per command.
- LaTeX via TinyTeX at `~/Library/TinyTeX/bin/universal-darwin/` — prepend to PATH if `which xelatex` is empty.

---

## 6. Preserve exploration code and inline notes during reorganization

Jacob is an exploratory researcher: many model variants exist, only one ships. Unused variants are research record, not garbage.

- **Classify, don't cull.** `canonical / current` vs. `prior-gen archive` vs. `out-of-scope` vs. `scratch`. Never `delete-candidate` unless Jacob says delete.
- "Superseded" = archive, not garbage.
- Don't strip inline R notes, section markers (`# ---- 1. Load data ----`), or interleaved prose during reorg. If consolidation would lose comments, propose a separate archive copy alongside the consolidated version.
- Frame reorg proposals as **additive structure** (new `MAP.md`, index, rename), not deletion of variants.
- Default to keeping. Ask before removing anything Jacob authored.

Origin: 2026-05-08, Education-Wellbeing canonical-folder mapping task.

central_files.manuscript-rules.md:
# Manuscript-project rules — WIP

A central, work-in-progress collection of rules that apply specifically to **manuscript projects** (papers being drafted toward journal/conference submission). Lab-agnostic — Jacob works across multiple labs and these rules apply to all of them.

Pull into a manuscript project's local `CLAUDE.md` either by copy-paste or by Claude Code's `@`-import syntax:

```
@${HOME}/Claude/manuscript-rules.md
```

This file is **evolving**. Add new rules as patterns emerge from manuscript work. Treat it as the canonical source — when manuscript rules change, edit here, and downstream projects pick up the change via import.

Last updated: 2026-05-11.

---

## 1. Faithful formatter, not co-author

Claude is a faithful formatter, **not** a co-author. Substantive content changes (rewording an argument, adding a citation, restructuring a section, changing a claim's hedge level) need explicit authorization from the human owner. Cosmetic and formatting changes (typos, spacing, capitalization, reference-list ordering, bibliography formatting) can go directly without asking.

When in doubt, ask. The cost of asking is small; the cost of silently rewriting a paragraph is high.

## 2. Don't police word counts mid-draft

Don't surface word counts during brainstorming or drafting. Only enforce when:

- Jacob explicitly asks for the word count
- The artifact is at submission stage (final formatting pass)
- A specific length limit is part of a rule Claude is checking (e.g., 120-word PNAS Significance Statement at final-format pass)

Default during creative work: **no word-count anxiety.** Many useful drafts run long; trimming is a separate pass.

## 3. Draft-version suffix: `_PROPOSED`

Manuscripts that Claude generates or substantially revises get a `_PROPOSED` suffix on the filename (e.g., `bottom_up_wellbeing_v6_PROPOSED.md`). Drop the suffix only when the human owner signs off on the version.

This differs from the `(claude)` suffix used for non-manuscript Claude artifacts (lit reviews, analysis notes, etc.). Manuscripts are the high-stakes case and get their own marker.

## 4. (Future rules — placeholder)

Add more as they come up. Likely candidates as the file matures:

- HANDOFF.md conventions for collaborator transitions
- Comment-roundtrip discipline (when responding to inline Word comments)
- Citation-verification thresholds before integrating a new reference
- Figure-versioning patterns (matching `_PROPOSED` for visuals)
- Submission-final formatting checklists by venue

---

*This file is imported / referenced from individual manuscript projects' local `CLAUDE.md`. Edits here propagate to every project that imports it.*

skills_user.name: citation-checker
skills_user.description: Verify academic citations against CrossRef, Semantic Scholar, and OpenAlex. Detects AI-hallucinated references, chimeric citations, and suspicious patterns.

skills_user.name: html-review-builder
skills_user.description: Build interactive HTML review reports for multi-turn collaboration with Jacob — the artifact Claude reopens after substantial updates per global CLAUDE.md §3. Different from `markdown-report-builder` (that one renders finished docs to .docx/.pdf). This skill is for live review artifacts that evolve

skills_user.name: last30days-skill
skills_user.description: Research what people actually say about any topic in the last 30 days. Pulls posts and engagement from Reddit, X, YouTube, TikTok, Hacker News, Polymarket, GitHub, and the web.

skills_user.name: markdown-report-builder
skills_user.description: Build polished `.docx` (via pandoc reference doc) and `.pdf` (via Eisvogel LaTeX template) from a markdown source. Cross-project; one-time setup per project, then `./reports/build.sh report.md` produces a clean Word + PDF pair. Use when Jacob asks to "render this report" / "build a polished version"

skills_user.name: paper-retrieval
skills_user.description: Use when the user asks to find or download the PDF of a specific named academic paper by title, author, or DOI. Triggers on "download paper X", "find/get the PDF of Y", "fetch this paper", "get the full text of Z", or upgrading an ABSTRACT-ONLY reference to FULL-PDF. Skip for general literature expl

skills_user.name: paper-search
skills_user.description: ALWAYS invoke this skill when the user asks to search/browse/find academic literature on a topic. Do NOT use WebSearch or general knowledge for "find papers on X" / "what's the literature on Y" / "recent work about Z" — use this skill first. Searches arXiv, PubMed, Semantic Scholar, CrossRef across

skills_user.name: research-mode
skills_user.description: Anti-hallucination research mode. Toggle on to enforce citation requirements, source grounding, and "I don't know" behavior. Toggle off for creative work.

skills_plugin.name: brainstorming
skills_plugin.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

skills_plugin.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skills_plugin.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

skills_plugin.name: executing-plans
skills_plugin.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

skills_plugin.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skills_plugin.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

skills_plugin.name: receiving-code-review
skills_plugin.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

skills_plugin.name: requesting-code-review
skills_plugin.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

skills_plugin.name: subagent-driven-development
skills_plugin.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

skills_plugin.name: systematic-debugging
skills_plugin.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

skills_plugin.name: test-driven-development
skills_plugin.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

skills_plugin.name: using-git-worktrees
skills_plugin.description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification

skills_plugin.name: using-superpowers
skills_plugin.description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions

skills_plugin.name: verification-before-completion
skills_plugin.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

skills_plugin.name: writing-plans
skills_plugin.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

skills_plugin.name: writing-skills
skills_plugin.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

skills_plugin.name: env-compare
skills_plugin.description: Fetch a collaborator's published Claude Code environment snapshot, diff it against the user's local environment, render an HTML diff report, and walk the user item-by-item through what to import — with Claude actively judging each conflict and recommending an action. Also surfaces things the local u

skills_plugin.name: publish-env-snapshot
skills_plugin.description: Capture a redacted snapshot of the user's Claude Code environment (settings.json, mcpServers, global CLAUDE.md, installed skills + commands) and publish it to a git repo so collaborators can compare against it. Use when the user says "publish my claude env", "publish my env snapshot", "update my env

skills_plugin.name: commented-edit-roundtrip
skills_plugin.description: Round-trip editing between Claude and the user using Word margin comments. Two modes — (A) PERPETUAL INBOX: a single .docx the user keeps adding margin comments to forever; Claude processes comments into TODOs without renaming the file, so the user is never locked out of their commenting surface; (B

skills_plugin.name: research-27
skills_plugin.description: Produce a credibly-sourced, citation-rich research brief on a user-supplied topic. Treats the response like a mini literature review — every major claim cited inline, mixed-media sources allowed when author/outlet credibility is established, and a verifiable APA-style reference list at the end with

skills_plugin.name: revision-queue
skills_plugin.description: Manage a multi-round revision workflow with two coordinated files — an open-discussion TODO list and an append-only chronological change log. Use when a user is iterating on a manuscript, codebase, or any artifact across many small decisions and wants to keep "what's open" and "what's done" cleanly

skills_plugin.name: sync-cowork-skill
skills_plugin.description: One-way publish of a Cowork-side skill (where Jacob actively edits and improves) to the public Xtzj2333/jacob-skills marketplace plugin (what Tony installs). Run from Claude Code only. Trigger on phrases like 'sync calendar-search to github', 'publish my cowork skill', 'push my <skill> to jacob-skil

skills_plugin.name: tony-github-push
skills_plugin.description: Push manuscript edits from a configured local subdirectory to a configured branch on a configured GitHub remote. **Trigger ONLY when the user explicitly says one of:** "tony github push", "tony and github push them", "push tony", "push to tony's github", "push to tony", or invokes "/tony-github-push

source_paths.central_files: ~/Claude/<filename>.md — lab-agnostic central rules files referenced by project-local CLAUDE.md via @-import; flat name→content map
source_paths.installed_plugins: ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json — per-plugin version, gitCommitSha, install path (gives strict version pinning)

notes: All known API-key shapes (sk-, tvly-, ghp_, AKIA, JWT, etc.) replaced with <REDACTED:type> tokens.
Object keys whose name contains api_key/token/secret/password/auth had their string values redacted.
Local home paths (/Users/<you>/) normalized to ${HOME} for portability.
Slash-command bodies are captured (redacted). Skill bodies are NOT — only name + description.
Plugin-shipped skills are listed with source='plugin:<plugin>@<marketplace>'; user-level with source='user'.
Forward-compat fields (agents, keybindings) may be empty if those directories don't exist on this machine.
If you spot anything sensitive that wasn't caught, edit this file by hand before committing.
